name: signed_div_missing_cqo
trigger: Signed quotient and remainder of negative dividend — must emit cqo before idivq.
causal_chain: AST sees /, % on signed long → IR emits SDIV/SREM → codegen lowers to cqo + idivq pair (rdx:rax = sign-extended dividend, idivq divisor → quotient in rax, remainder in rdx). Missing cqo: rdx contains stale data, idivq computes wrong quotient. Missing sign extension: divq treats -30 as unsigned 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE2.
suggested_fix: (reference behavior) IR codegen for signed division must emit cqo (or cqto) before idivq; for unsigned, must zero rdx with xor before divq.
counterexamples_tried: unsigned a / unsigned b — uses divq with rdx=0; different code path
positive signed dividend — works without cqo by accident if rdx happens to be 0
remaining_uncertainty: a buggy ZCC that elides cqo will produce a huge wrong quotient → exit 1 (divergent). reference produces exit 0.
